message:
scripts(symbol_fixture_coverage): write generated output to target/conformance

`scripts/check_symbol_fixture_coverage.sh` previously emitted its
intermediate coverage JSON to a `mktemp(1)` file and deleted it on
EXIT via a trap. That made debugging a failed run impossible —
whenever the script bailed with a mismatch, the most useful
artifact (the just-generated coverage snapshot) had already been
deleted by the exit trap before the user could inspect it.

This change:

- Creates (or reuses) `$ROOT/target/conformance/` up-front with
  `mkdir -p`. This directory is already git-ignored via the
  existing `target/` rule, so the file is not accidentally
  committed.
- Writes the generated output to a named file
  `target/conformance/symbol_fixture_coverage.generated.$$.v1.json`
  so concurrent script runs (different shells, parallel CI) each
  get a unique path via the `$$` PID suffix.
- Drops the `trap 'rm -f "${tmp}"' EXIT` cleanup. Persisting the
  file across exits is the whole point — if the script fails, a
  developer can open the exact JSON the comparison was run
  against. Disk pressure from accumulated generated files is
  bounded by the `target/` clean target anyone already runs.

Behaviour is otherwise unchanged: the same jq/python pipeline
produces the same JSON shape, the same pass/fail gate fires, the
same exit codes. Only the storage location and lifetime of the
intermediate file differ.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
